title: Alone vs Lonely
quote: I came to the conclusion that I would take actually being alone over feeling lonely in a relationship every day of the week.
excerpt: I came to the conclusion that I would take actually being alone over feeling lonely in a relationship every day of the week.
reflection: This was the moment I realized I'd been confusing companionship with connection. Being in a relationship where you feel invisible, unheard, or constantly second-guessing yourself isn't better than being single. It's worse. At least when you're alone, you're not being gaslit into thinking the problem is you. At least when you're alone, you can start to remember who you are.
fullText: This was the moment I realized I'd been confusing companionship with connection. Being in a relationship where you feel invisible, unheard, or constantly second-guessing yourself isn't better than being single. It's worse. At least when you're alone, you're not being gaslit into thinking the problem is you. At least when you're alone, you can start to remember who you are. It took me years to learn this, but once I did, I stopped settling for crumbs disguised as love.

title: Jurassic Park
quote: Dating was a walk in the park. Jurassic Park.
excerpt: Dating was a walk in the park. Jurassic Park.
reflection: Dating as a single mom in your 30s is less about romance and more about survival. The men who were functional, independent, intelligent, and emotionally astute had already been snatched up. The ones available? Well, let's just say their short arms couldn't reach their wallets when it was important, and their big teeth had no trouble chewing you up and spitting you out after they'd had their fill.
fullText: Dating as a single mom in your 30s is less about romance and more about survival. The men who were functional, independent, intelligent, and emotionally astute had already been snatched up. The ones available? Well, let's just say their short arms couldn't reach their wallets when it was important, and their big teeth had no trouble chewing you up and spitting you out after they'd had their fill. Finding someone who saw me as a person – not a mirror, a muse, or an emotional mule – felt like navigating a theme park where the attractions were actively trying to eat you.

title: Failing Fabulously
quote: I wasn't a failure. I was fabulous! Or I was failing fabulously.
excerpt: I wasn't a failure. I was fabulous! Or I was failing fabulously.
reflection: I gained 20 kilograms after a breakup. I went from a size 8 to a size 14. And you know what? It didn't bother me all that much. Because I finally realized that my value wasn't tied to my dress size, my relationship status, or my ability to maintain a 'perfect' life. I was sharp, perceptive, funny, and a damn good mom. That's what mattered. The rest? Just noise.
fullText: I gained 20 kilograms after a breakup. I went from a size 8 to a size 14. And you know what? It didn't bother me all that much. Because I finally realized that my value wasn't tied to my dress size, my relationship status, or my ability to maintain a 'perfect' life. I was sharp, perceptive, funny, and a damn good mom. That's what mattered. The rest? Just noise. At long last I realized that all that truly mattered in life was to love, to laugh, and to find joy wherever we could. And this was me loving myself at my 'worst.'

title: On Woolworths Bras and Seatbelts Disguised as Straps
excerpt: Thirty is the year your lingerie stops being flirtation and starts being infrastructure. The day I realized my bra was essentially a seatbelt for my tits was the day I understood I'd crossed a threshold.

title: Turning Thirty and Other Acts of Defiance
excerpt: I turned thirty in a pandemic, alone, with a toddler who'd just discovered the word 'no' and a cat who'd discovered the joy of knocking things off shelves. It was less a celebration and more a survival milestone.

title: Dating Apps and the Art of Low Expectations
excerpt: There's something deeply unsexy about swiping through profiles while your child asks why the man in the photo has a fish. Dating as a single parent is less romance, more logistics.

title: My Body at Thirty: A User's Manual
excerpt: My body and I have reached an understanding. It does its job, I try not to hate it, and we both pretend the stretch marks are tiger stripes. It's a truce, not a love story.
